steps.title: Create Medical Specialty
steps.note: Screenshot reused from the earlier run — specialty state unchanged.

steps.title: Create ReqForm
steps.done: Filename 'cgx.pdf' is misleading: pypdf detection returned nothing (image-only flattened PDF), so the PDF was read VISUALLY with Claude's Read tool. Content is the AMEDiX Immunodeficiency requisition form -> Short Code IMMUNO, Lab Amedix. Created ReqForm 'IMMUNO - Amedix 2026' (id 76017aff), gid 429018889, Is Default off. Name set to full Description directly (avoids the known HTTP 500 when Name = Lab).
steps.note: Intentional DUPLICATE: an identical 'IMMUNO - Amedix 2026' ReqForm already existed from earlier this session. User chose 'Process anyway'. Name corrected from PDF content, NOT from the 'cgx' filename.

steps.title: Sync CSV
steps.done: Ran 'Sync CSV' on the new ReqForm (id 76017aff).
steps.note: Confirmation: 'Syncing process has been started! You can look over it from Sequence Tracker'.

steps.title: Flatten PDF
steps.done: Flattened cgx.pdf with Ghostscript 10.05 (pdfimage24 @300dpi, 2 pages) to 'cgx-flatten-pdf.pdf'. (Source was already rasterized; re-flatten is harmless.)

steps.title: Import PDF
steps.done: Uploaded 'cgx-flatten-pdf.pdf' via Import PDF against the new ReqForm. The uploader listed only the new ReqForm (the older one already had a PDF, so it was filtered out) -> unambiguous selection. Verified Medical PDF record (Type Reqform) with S3 URL.

steps.title: Create Form.io order services form
steps.done: Content identical to the earlier recform, so the order-services form '[DEV] [AI] order services Immuno Amedix' (4 panels, SCID dropped) already covers it. Kept as-is (no new form). Linked its schema URL into the new ReqForm metadata (order_form_link).

steps.title: Final confirm
steps.done: Reviewed: new ReqForm (id 76017aff) with order_form_link metadata saved, and the uploaded Medical PDF 'cgx-flatten-pdf.pdf' present.
